MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

(Mini Minutes, continued from previous entry)
Further case management conference on August 17, 2016 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on October 3, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 29, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Leticia A. Ralls of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10070 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jun-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Robert Brown
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to reporter's transcript for appearances on behalf of defendants BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC, Pacific Coast Building Products, Simi Management Corp., Georgia-Pacific LLC, Honeywell International, Inc., James E. Marsh, Inc., Genuine Parts Company)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d). Trial date is October 3, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. 120th day is October 6, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 22, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 19, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 2, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 26, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on September 29, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 16, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 30, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 8, 2016. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than June 22, 2016 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than June 22, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)
